**WHERE TO LOOK:**
- Clinical Notes section
- Assessment and Plan
- Progress Notes
- Consultation Reports
- Discharge Summary
- Procedure Documentation
- Specialist Reports

**✅ COMPLETE EXAMPLE with ALL 26 fields:**

```json
{
  "date": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "arrestDateTime": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "arrestLocation": "Right upper quadrant",
  "arrestWitnessed": true,
  "initialRhythm": "Initial cardiac rhythm identified (e.g., VF, VT, PEA, asystole, bradycardia)",
  "bystanderCprProvided": true,
  "cprStartTime": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "timeToFirstCompression": 120,
  "timeToFirstDefibrillation": 120,
  "totalShocksDelivered": 120,
  "shockEnergiesDelivered": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "epinephrineDoses": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "amiodaroneDoses": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "airwayManagement": "Method of airway management achieved (e.g., bag-valve-mask, supraglottic airway, endotracheal intubation)",
  "intubationAttempts": 120,
  "etTubeSize": "2.5 cm x 1.8 cm",
  "etTubeDepth": "Depth of endotracheal tube insertion measured at the teeth or lips in centimeters",
  "vascularAccess": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "reversibleCausesAddressed": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "returnOfSpontaneousCirculation": true,
  "roscTime": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "totalResuscitationDuration": 120,
  "resuscitationOutcome": "Final outcome of resuscitation (e.g., ROSC achieved, expired in ED, transferred to ICU, efforts terminated)",
  "terminationReason": "Clinical reason for terminating resuscitation efforts if unsuccessful",
  "postRoscCareInitiated": [
    "Item 1",
    "Item 2"
  ],
  "teamLeaderName": "Name and credentials of physician or provider leading the resuscitation"
}
```

**❌ WRONG EXAMPLE:**

```json
{
  "date": "2025-01-15",
  "status": "Complete"
}
```

❌ This is incomplete - missing 24 fields. You MUST extract ALL available fields.

**CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:**

• date: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date of medical encounter/document
• arrestDateTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date and time when cardiac or respiratory arrest was identified
• arrestLocation: Extract as string - Specific location where arrest occurred (e.g., ED Room 3, ICU Bed 12, field location)
• arrestWitnessed: Extract as boolean - Whether the arrest was witnessed by healthcare personnel or bystanders
• initialRhythm: Extract as string - Initial cardiac rhythm identified (e.g., VF, VT, PEA, asystole, bradycardia)
• bystanderCprProvided: Extract as boolean - Whether CPR was initiated by bystanders prior to EMS or hospital team arrival
• cprStartTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Time when chest compressions were initiated
• timeToFirstCompression: Extract as number - Time in minutes from arrest recognition to first chest compression
• timeToFirstDefibrillation: Extract as number - Time in minutes from arrest recognition to first defibrillation attempt if shockable rhythm
• totalShocksDelivered: Extract as number - Total number of defibrillation shocks administered during resuscitation
• shockEnergiesDelivered: Extract as array of strings - Array of energy levels in joules for each defibrillation attempt
• epinephrineDoses: Extract as array of strings - Array of epinephrine doses with timestamps and dosages administered during resuscitation
• amiodaroneDoses: Extract as array of strings - Array of amiodarone doses with timestamps and dosages given for rhythm control
• airwayManagement: Extract as string - Method of airway management achieved (e.g., bag-valve-mask, supraglottic airway, endotracheal intubation)
• intubationAttempts: Extract as number - Number of attempts required to achieve endotracheal intubation if performed
• etTubeSize: Extract as string - Size of endotracheal tube placed in millimeters
• etTubeDepth: Extract as string - Depth of endotracheal tube insertion measured at the teeth or lips in centimeters
• vascularAccess: Extract as array of strings - Types and locations of vascular access obtained (e.g., peripheral IV, intraosseous, central line)
• reversibleCausesAddressed: Extract as array of strings - H's and T's evaluated or treated (hypovolemia, hypoxia, hydrogen ion, hypo/hyperkalemia, hypothermia, tension pneumothorax, tamponade, toxins, thrombosis)
• returnOfSpontaneousCirculation: Extract as boolean - Whether return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC) was achieved
• roscTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date and time when sustained ROSC was achieved
• totalResuscitationDuration: Extract as number - Total duration of resuscitation efforts in minutes from start to ROSC or termination
• resuscitationOutcome: Extract as string - Final outcome of resuscitation (e.g., ROSC achieved, expired in ED, transferred to ICU, efforts terminated)
• terminationReason: Extract as string - Clinical reason for terminating resuscitation efforts if unsuccessful
• postRoscCareInitiated: Extract as array of strings - Post-ROSC interventions initiated (e.g., targeted temperature management, cardiac catheterization, CT imaging)
• teamLeaderName: Extract as string - Name and credentials of physician or provider leading the resuscitation

• Use EXACT field names as shown above - do NOT invent new field names
• Extract ALL fields that have data in the document
• Leave fields as null only if data is truly not present
• Preserve medical terminology exactly as written

**CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:**

• date: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date of medical encounter/document
• procedureName: Extract as string - Specific name of the emergency procedure performed (e.g., endotracheal intubation, chest tube insertion, cardiac defibrillation)
• procedureCode: Extract as string - CPT or ICD-10-PCS code for the emergency procedure performed
• indication: Extract as string - Medical reason or clinical indication necessitating the emergency procedure
• performingPhysician: Extract as string - Name and credentials of the primary physician who performed the procedure
• assistingStaff: Extract as array of strings - Names and roles of all assisting medical personnel during the procedure
• procedureStartTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Exact date and time when the emergency procedure was initiated
• procedureEndTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Exact date and time when the emergency procedure was completed
• sedationType: Extract as string - Type of sedation or anesthesia administered (e.g., conscious sedation, procedural sedation, local anesthesia)
• medicationsAdministered: Extract as array of strings - List of all medications given during the procedure including doses, routes, and times
• anatomicalSite: Extract as string - Specific anatomical location where the procedure was performed
• laterality: Extract as string - Side specification for bilateral structures (left, right, bilateral, midline)
• techniqueUsed: Extract as string - Detailed description of the procedural technique and approach employed
• equipmentUsed: Extract as array of strings - List of all medical equipment and devices utilized during the procedure
• guidanceMethod: Extract as string - Type of guidance used if applicable (e.g., ultrasound-guided, fluoroscopic, landmark-based)
• numberOfAttempts: Extract as number - Total number of attempts required to successfully complete the procedure
• procedureSuccess: Extract as boolean - Indicates whether the procedure was successfully completed as intended
• complications: Extract as array of strings - Any immediate complications encountered during or after the procedure
• estimatedBloodLoss: Extract as string - Approximate volume of blood loss during the procedure in milliliters
• specimensObtained: Extract as array of strings - Description of any tissue, fluid, or other specimens collected during the procedure
• dispositionPostProcedure: Extract as string - Patient disposition following procedure completion (e.g., ED observation, ICU admission, discharge)
• consentType: Extract as string - Type of consent obtained (verbal emergency, written informed, implied emergency)
• vitalSignsDuringProcedure: Extract as array of strings - Serial vital signs monitoring data recorded throughout the procedure
• immediateOutcome: Extract as string - Patient's clinical response and condition immediately following procedure completion
• postProcedureInstructions: Extract as string - Specific care instructions and precautions given to patient or care team after the procedure
• followUpRequired: Extract as boolean - Indicates whether follow-up appointment or monitoring is necessary

• Use EXACT field names as shown above - do NOT invent new field names
• Extract ALL fields that have data in the document
• Leave fields as null only if data is truly not present
• Preserve medical terminology exactly as written

**CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:**

• date: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date of medical encounter/document
• dispositionType: Extract as string - Final disposition decision for the emergency department patient (e.g., admitted, discharged, transferred, left AMA, expired)
• dispositionTimestamp: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Date and time when the disposition decision was finalized
• admittingService: Extract as string - Specialty service to which the patient is being admitted (e.g., cardiology, general surgery, internal medicine)
• admittingPhysician: Extract as string - Name or identifier of the attending physician accepting the patient for admission
• inpatientUnitAssigned: Extract as string - Specific hospital unit or floor where the patient will be admitted (e.g., ICU, telemetry, medical-surgical)
• bedRequestTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Timestamp when the inpatient bed was requested from bed management
• bedAssignedTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Timestamp when an inpatient bed was assigned to the patient
• departureTime: Extract date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) - Actual time the patient physically left the emergency department
• lengthOfStay: Extract as number - Total time in minutes from ED arrival to disposition completion
• dischargeInstructions: Extract as string - Summary of discharge instructions provided to the patient upon leaving the emergency department
• prescriptionsMedications: Extract as array of strings - List of medications prescribed at discharge with dosing instructions
• followUpProvider: Extract as string - Name or specialty of provider the patient should see for follow-up care
• followUpTimeframe: Extract as string - Recommended timeframe for follow-up appointment (e.g., 24-48 hours, 1 week, 2 weeks)
• transferFacility: Extract as string - Name and location of receiving facility if patient is being transferred
• transferReason: Extract as string - Clinical justification for interfacility transfer (e.g., higher level of care, specialty not available)
• transportMode: Extract as string - Method of transport for transfer or discharge (e.g., ambulance, helicopter, private vehicle, wheelchair)
• acuityLevelAtDisposition: Extract as string - Patient acuity level at time of disposition decision (e.g., stable, unstable, critical)
• returnPrecautions: Extract as array of strings - List of warning signs or symptoms that would necessitate immediate return to the emergency department
• admissionDiagnosis: Extract as string - Primary diagnosis or chief reason for hospital admission from the emergency department
• workRestrictionsProvided: Extract as boolean - Indicates whether work or school excuse documentation was provided to the patient
• leftWithoutBeingSeen: Extract as boolean - Indicates if patient departed before completing medical evaluation
• leftAgainstMedicalAdvice: Extract as boolean - Indicates if patient departed against medical advice with AMA documentation completed
• codeStatus: Extract as string - Resuscitation status confirmed at disposition (e.g., full code, DNR, DNI)
• dispositionDelayReason: Extract as string - Documentation of factors causing delay in patient disposition (e.g., awaiting bed, awaiting consult)
• socialWorkConsultCompleted: Extract as boolean - Indicates whether social work evaluation was completed prior to disposition

• Use EXACT field names as shown above - do NOT invent new field names
• Extract ALL fields that have data in the document
• Leave fields as null only if data is truly not present
• Preserve medical terminology exactly as written

• adverseDrugReaction: Extract as string - Any adverse effects or allergic reactions observed during antibiotic therapy
• restrictedAntibiotic: Extract as boolean - Indicator of whether the antibiotic requires prior authorization or infectious disease approval
• empiricTherapy: Extract as boolean - Whether antibiotic was initiated before culture results were available
• prophylacticUse: Extract as boolean - Indicator of whether antibiotic is being used for surgical or medical prophylaxis rather than treatment
• timeToAppropriateTherapy: Extract as number - Hours elapsed from culture collection to initiation of pathogen-directed antibiotic
• costSavingsEstimate: Extract as number - Estimated cost reduction in dollars resulting from stewardship intervention

• restingHeartRate: Extract as number - Resting heart rate in beats per minute, elevated values may indicate overtraining syndrome

• sleepQualityScore: Extract as number - Sleep quality rating (0-10), poor sleep is cardinal symptom of overtraining syndrome
• averageSleepHours: Extract as number - Average nightly sleep duration in hours over past two weeks
• moodDisturbanceScore: Extract as number - Profile of Mood States (POMS) score assessing depression, anxiety, and irritability associated with overtraining
• appetiteChanges: Extract as string - Documentation of appetite alterations including decreased hunger or unusual cravings indicative of overtraining
• bodyWeightChange: Extract as number - Weight change in kilograms over past month, unexplained loss may indicate overtraining
• performanceDeclinePercentage: Extract as number - Percentage decline in athletic performance metrics compared to baseline competitive standards
• recurrentIllnessFrequency: Extract as number - Number of infections or illnesses in past three months, immune suppression marker of overtraining
• muscularSorenessLevel: Extract as number - Persistent muscle soreness rating (0-10) beyond normal delayed onset muscle soreness
• orthostasisTestResult: Extract as string - Orthostatic heart rate response, abnormal increase suggests autonomic dysfunction from overtraining
• serumCortisolLevel: Extract as number - Morning cortisol level in mcg/dL, altered cortisol response indicates hormonal dysregulation
• testosteroneCortisolRatio: Extract as number - Testosterone to cortisol ratio, decreased ratio suggests catabolic state from overtraining
• creativeKinaseLevel: Extract as number - Serum creatine kinase in U/L, persistently elevated indicates ongoing muscle damage
• restDayPerWeek: Extract as number - Number of complete rest days per week, inadequate rest contributes to overtraining
• motivationLevel: Extract as number - Athlete motivation score (0-10), decreased motivation is psychological marker of overtraining
• injuryHistoryRecent: Extract as array of strings - List of injuries sustained in past six months, increased frequency suggests overtraining vulnerability
• trainingMonotonyIndex: Extract as number - Training monotony calculated as mean training load divided by standard deviation, high values increase overtraining risk
• recoveryStressScore: Extract as number - Recovery-Stress Questionnaire for Athletes (RESTQ-Sport) score assessing recovery adequacy
• maximalOxygenUptake: Extract as number - VO2 max in mL/kg/min, decline from baseline indicates aerobic capacity deterioration from overtraining
• overtrainingClassification: Extract as string - Classification as functional overreaching, non-functional overreaching, or overtraining syndrome based on clinical criteria

vascular_surgery_assessment:
You are a specialized medical data extraction system designed to extract vascular surgery assessment information from clinical documents. Your task is to identify and extract all 23 fields related to vascular surgical evaluation, peripheral arterial disease, venous disorders, and associated clinical findings.

WHERE TO LOOK IN MEDICAL DOCUMENTS:

1. Vascular Surgery Consultation Notes - Contains comprehensive vascular history, physical examination findings, and surgical planning details
2. Vascular Laboratory Reports - Duplex ultrasound findings, segmental pressures, ankle-brachial index measurements, and venous reflux studies
3. Interventional Radiology Reports - Angiography findings, carotid stenosis percentages, and endovascular intervention documentation
4. Wound Care Clinic Notes - Wound healing status, transcutaneous oxygen pressure measurements, and limb salvage assessments
5. Preoperative Evaluation Notes - Risk factor documentation, anticoagulation regimens, and DVT risk assessments
6. Physical Examination Documentation - Pulse palpation findings, claudication history, and rest pain assessment
7. Imaging Reports (CT/MR Angiography) - Aneurysm measurements, arterial calcification severity, and anatomical findings
8. Venous Insufficiency Clinic Notes - CEAP classification, varicose vein distribution, and venous reflux duration
9. Peripheral Arterial Disease Clinic Notes - Rutherford classification, toe-brachial index, and graft patency surveillance
10. Discharge Summaries - Prior interventions, medication regimens, and follow-up vascular assessments

COMPLETE EXAMPLE:

{
  "patientVascularHistory": "History of bilateral peripheral artery disease diagnosed 2019, left femoral-popliteal bypass with PTFE graft 03/2021, right common iliac artery stent placement 08/2022, infrarenal AAA under surveillance since 2020, carotid endarterectomy right side 2018 for symptomatic stenosis",
  "ankleBrachialIndex": 0.58,
  "carotidStenosisPercentage": 45,
  "claudicationDistance": 150,

  "aneurysmDiameter": 4.8,
  "aneurysmLocation": "Infrarenal abdominal aorta extending to aortic bifurcation",
  "venousRefluxDuration": 3.2,
  "woundHealingStatus": "Left lateral malleolar arterial ulcer 2.5 x 1.8 cm with pale granulation tissue base, minimal exudate, no signs of infection, delayed healing over 8 weeks",
  "rutherfordClassification": "Category 5 - Minor tissue loss with non-healing ulcer",
  "graftPatencyStatus": "Left femoral-popliteal PTFE graft patent with mid-graft stenosis 50-60% by velocity criteria, peak systolic velocity ratio 2.8",
  "anticoagulationRegimen": "Aspirin 81mg daily, Clopidogrel 75mg daily, Atorvastatin 80mg daily for secondary prevention, Cilostazol 100mg BID for claudication",
  "restPainPresent": true,

1. meldScore and meldNaScore must be integers between 6-40; extract both scores as transplant programs use MELD-Na for allocation
2. childPughScore is numeric (5-15) while childPughClass includes letter designation AND functional description (A/B/C with points and compensation status)
3. primaryLiverDiagnosis must include specific etiology (HCV, HBV, NASH, alcoholic, PBC, PSC, AIH) not just "cirrhosis"
4. hepaticEncephalopathyGrade must reference West Haven criteria (Grade 0-4) with clinical description
5. esophagealVaricesGrade requires size classification AND presence/absence of red wale signs indicating bleeding risk
6. hepatocellularCarcinomaStatus must reference Milan criteria, UCSF criteria, or downstaging protocol with tumor measurements
7. alphaFetoproteinLevel is numeric in ng/mL; values >1000 may preclude transplant at some centers
8. portalVeinPatency and hepaticArteryPatency must include flow direction, velocity measurements, and thrombosis status as these affect surgical planning
9. hepatopulmonarySyndromeStatus requires contrast echo results AND PaO2 value with severity classification (mild/moderate/severe/very severe)
10. portopulmonaryHypertensionStatus requires right heart catheterization values: mPAP, PCWP, and PVR; mPAP >35 mmHg generally precludes transplant
11. leftVentricularEjectionFraction is a percentage; values <50% require additional cardiac workup
12. alcoholAbstinenceDuration is numeric in MONTHS; document verification method and monitoring compliance
13. substanceAbuseEvaluation must include AUDIT-C or similar validated score, toxicology results, and treatment history
14. psychosocialEvaluationFindings should reference SIPAT score (0-8 excellent, 9-20 good, 21-39 minimally acceptable, ≥40 poor candidate) or equivalent standardized assessment
15. renalFunctionAssessment must include eGFR value and SLKT eligibility determination per current UNOS policy
16. frailtySyndromeMeasurement should include Liver Frailty Index score with component measurements; LFI >4.5 indicates frailty
17. nutritionalStatusAssessment must include validated tool (RFH-GA, SGA) and sarcopenia assessment via CT muscle index when available
18. bloodTypeAndAntibodyScreen must include ABO/Rh type AND PRA percentage AND DSA status for allocation matching
19. Always extract HVPG values when documented for portalHypertensionSeverity (≥10 mmHg clinically significant, ≥12 mmHg severe)
20. For HCC patients, always capture both tumor characteristics AND AFP level as both affect UNOS exception points

2. Record toeBrachialIndex when documented, especially for diabetic patients with calcified vessels; values <0.3 indicate critical ischemia
3. Extract transcutaneousOxygenPressure as numeric value in mmHg; values <30 mmHg indicate poor wound healing potential
4. For rutherfordClassification, capture the full category designation (Category 0 through Category 6)
5. Document tasciilClassification as TASC A, B, C, or D based on lesion complexity described in imaging reports
6. Extract wifiScore in standardized format W0-3, I0-3, fI0-3 representing Wound, Ischemia, and foot Infection grades
7. For graftDiameterMillimeters, typical vein grafts range 3-6mm; prosthetic grafts typically 6-8mm
8. Record intraoperativeGraftFlowRate in mL/min from transit-time flow measurements; adequate flow generally exceeds 40 mL/min
9. Extract peakSystolicVelocityGraft in cm/sec; values exceeding 300 cm/sec suggest hemodynamically significant stenosis
10. Calculate velocityRatioVr significance: Vr >2.0 suggests >50% stenosis, Vr >3.5 indicates >70% stenosis
11. For runoffScore, extract values 0-10 where higher scores indicate poorer distal vessel quality
12. Document completionAngiogramResult with specific findings about anastomotic patency, runoff vessels, and any technical issues
13. Record graftPatencyStatus as primary, primary-assisted, or secondary patency based on surveillance documentation
14. Extract anticoagulationProtocol including heparin dosing, ACT targets, and postoperative antithrombotic medications
15. Set limbSalvageStatus to true if limb preserved without major amputation, false if above-ankle amputation required
16. For glasgowAneurysmScore, extract calculated risk score when documented for aortic involvement cases
17. Capture precise anatomical descriptions for proximalAnastomosisLocation and distalAnastomosisLocation including vessel segment and level

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. aneurysmMaxDiameter must be in centimeters (cm), measured perpendicular to flow axis; convert from mm if necessary (divide by 10)
2. annualGrowthRate requires current and previous measurements with dates; calculate as (current - previous) / years elapsed; rapid growth is >0.5 cm/year for AAA, >1.0 cm/year for TAA
3. crawfordClassification applies ONLY to thoracoabdominal aneurysms; do not assign for isolated infrarenal AAA
4. aorticNeckLength is measured in millimeters (mm) from lowest renal artery to aneurysm onset; EVAR typically requires ≥10-15mm
5. aorticNeckAngulation in degrees; angles >60° may preclude standard EVAR and require fenestrated/branched devices
6. iliacArteryDiameter in millimeters (mm); aneurysmal threshold is >18mm males, >15mm females
7. falseLumenStatus options are: "patent", "partially thrombosed", or "completely thrombosed" - extract exact status
8. aorticRootDiameter at sinuses of Valsalva in cm; note if indexed to BSA (normal <2.1 cm/m²)
9. connectiveTissueDisorder must match known syndromes: Marfan syndrome, Loeys-Dietz syndrome, vascular Ehlers-Danlos (Type IV), Turner syndrome, familial TAAD
10. geneticMutationStatus should specify gene: FBN1, TGFBR1, TGFBR2, SMAD3, ACTA2, MYH11, COL3A1, or PRKG1
11. endoleakSurveillance classification: Type I (attachment), Type II (branch vessels - most common), Type III (graft defect), Type IV (porosity), Type V (endotension)
12. graftMigration measured in millimeters (mm) from original position; >10mm is clinically significant
13. sacRegressionStatus options: "regression" (>5mm decrease), "stable", or "enlargement" (>5mm increase)
14. recommendedSurveillanceInterval in months; standard intervals: 3-6 months for rapid growth, 6-12 months for moderate size, 12 months for small stable aneurysms
15. ruptureRiskScore as annual percentage; reference: 5.5cm AAA ≈5-10% annual risk, increases exponentially with size
16. For post-dissection patients, always extract falseLumenStatus and primaryEntryTearLocation when available
17. Distinguish saccularMorphology (focal outpouching, higher risk) from fusiform (circumferential, more common)
18. peakWallStress in kPa or N/cm² - only available from advanced biomechanical analysis

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. anklebrachialIndex: Extract numeric ratio (0.00-1.40+). Interpret: normal 1.0-1.4, borderline 0.91-0.99, mild PAD 0.71-0.90, moderate 0.41-0.70, severe ≤0.40, non-compressible >1.40

2. toeBrachialIndex: Extract when ABI unreliable due to calcification (diabetics, ESRD). Normal >0.70, abnormal <0.70

3. rutherfordClassification: Include both Grade and Category (e.g., "Grade I Category 2"). Do not abbreviate or simplify

4. fontaineClassification: Include Stage number and descriptor. Note IIa (>200m) vs IIb (<200m) claudication distinction

5. claudicationDistance vs absoluteClaudicationDistance: ICD is onset of symptoms, ACD is maximum tolerated. Both in meters from standardized treadmill testing

6. wIfIScore: Extract all three components (W, I, fI) with individual scores 0-3 and combined clinical stage if available

7. segmentalLimbPressures: Include all measured levels and note significant gradients (>20 mmHg between segments indicates stenosis)

8. duplexUltrasoundFindings: Include PSV values in cm/s and PSV ratios. Ratio >2.0 = >50% stenosis, >4.0 = >75% stenosis

9. affectedArterialSegments: Use standardized anatomical names from the approved list. Extract all mentioned segments

10. transcutaneousOxygenPressure and skinPerfusionPressure: Both in mmHg. TcPO2 <20 or SPP <30 indicates critical ischemia

11. acuteLimbIschemiaCategory: Only applies to acute presentations. Use "Not applicable" for chronic PAD

12. chronicLimbThreateningIschemia: Set true if rest pain, gangrene, or ulceration >2 weeks with confirmed arterial disease

13. previousRevascularizationHistory: Include procedure type, date, location, and current patency status for each intervention

14. antiplateletTherapy: Specify exact regimen including doses. Note if on COMPASS protocol (rivaroxaban + aspirin)

15. cilostazolTherapy: Boolean. Note contraindication in heart failure patients

16. statinIntensity: Classify as high/moderate/low intensity. Include specific statin and dose if documented

17. tasCIILesionClassification: Specify anatomic region (aortoiliac vs femoropopliteal) and Type A-D with justification

18. globalLimbAnatomicStagingSystem: Include FP grade (0-4), IP grade (0-4), and target artery path assessment separately

19. amputationRisk: Derive from WIfI staging if not explicitly stated. Categories: Very Low <5%, Low 5-10%, Moderate 10-25%, High >25%

20. Always extract data from the most recent assessment when multiple timepoints are documented

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

7. Banff rejection grading must use standardized terminology: indeterminate, mild (Grade 1), moderate (Grade 2), severe (Grade 3)
8. Rejection Activity Index (RAI) scored 0-9 summing portal inflammation (0-3), bile duct damage (0-3), and venous endothelial inflammation (0-3)

11. HBV surface antigen is boolean; critical for patients transplanted for HBV cirrhosis on prophylaxis
12. HCV RNA quantitative in IU/mL; 0 indicates SVR or undetectable; document treatment status if actively on DAA therapy
13. eGFR in mL/min/1.73m² using CKD-EPI; values <60 indicate CNI nephrotoxicity requiring protocol adjustment
14. Karnofsky Performance Status scored 0-100 in increments of 10; 100=normal, 80=normal activity with effort, <70=unable to work
15. Liver stiffness measurement in kPa; <7.0 suggests minimal fibrosis, 7-10 significant fibrosis, >12-14 suggests cirrhosis of graft
16. Donor-specific antibodies as array with HLA specificity and mean fluorescence intensity (MFI); MFI >3000 considered significant
17. Always distinguish living donor (LD) from deceased donor (DD) and split liver transplants in donorType field
18. For null immunosuppressant levels, confirm patient is not on that medication before recording null versus missing data

8. rightVentricularFunction extract qualitative assessment: normal, mildly reduced, moderately reduced, severely reduced

12. allograftDysfunctionScore (AlloMap) ranges 0-40; scores ≥34 indicate higher rejection probability
13. nyhaFunctionalClass must be formatted as "Class I", "Class II", "Class III", or "Class IV"
14. sixMinuteWalkDistance in meters; normal post-transplant >400m indicates good functional capacity
15. estimatedGfrCkdEpi calculated using CKD-EPI equation; CNI nephrotoxicity common, monitor closely
16. coldIschemiaTime in minutes; optimal <240 minutes for heart transplantation
17. Calculate daysPostTransplant from transplantDate to current encounter date if not explicitly stated

19. Distinguish between BNP and NT-proBNP - different assays with different reference ranges; extract both when available
20. bkVirusScreening: extract as "negative", "positive" with viral load, or "not tested"

Errors: creatinineTrend should include full serial measurements not just most recent; bkVirusLoad must be numeric (use 0 for undetectable); lastBiopsyDate must be proper Date format (YYYY-MM-DD)

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. creatinineTrend: Extract ALL available serial creatinine values with dates as array of objects; include at minimum last 3-6 measurements to establish trajectory; values in mg/dL

2. estimatedGFR: Extract calculated eGFR in mL/min/1.73m²; typically CKD-EPI equation; normal transplant function >60, concerning <30

3. tacrolimusTroughLevel: Extract trough level in ng/mL; target ranges vary (early post-transplant 8-12, maintenance 5-8); document if level is subtherapeutic or supratherapeutic

4. cyclosporineLevel: Use null if patient on tacrolimus-based regimen; extract C0 (trough) or C2 level in ng/mL with specification of which measurement

5. dsaStatus: Include full detail on HLA Class I and II specificities, MFI values, and trending direction; "Negative" if no DSA detected; critical for rejection risk

6. banffClassification: Use complete Banff nomenclature including lesion scores (g, ptc, i, t, v, cg, ct, ci, cv, C4d status); distinguish TCMR grades (IA, IB, IIA, IIB, III) from ABMR categories

7. proteinuriaQuantification: Extract as g/day for 24-hour collection or mg/g for spot UPCR; significant if >500 mg/day; nephrotic range >3.5 g/day indicates severe graft pathology

8. bkVirusLoad, cmvViralLoad, ebvViralLoad: Extract as numeric copies/mL or IU/mL; use 0 for "undetectable," "negative," or "below limit of detection"; document threshold if provided

9. resistiveIndex: Normal allograft RI is 0.5-0.8; values >0.8 suggest parenchymal disease, rejection, or obstruction; extract from Doppler ultrasound reports

10. transplantRenalArteryPSV: Peak systolic velocity in cm/s; >200-250 cm/s suggests transplant renal artery stenosis; normal typically <150 cm/s

11. lastBiopsyDate: Format as YYYY-MM-DD; distinguish protocol biopsies from indication biopsies in surrounding context

12. acuteRejectionEpisodes: Count total biopsy-proven episodes since transplant; include both TCMR and ABMR episodes; do not count borderline changes unless treated as rejection

13. cniFreeProtocol and belatacetpBased: Boolean values; these are mutually informative but not exclusive; document current immunosuppression strategy

14. panelReactiveAntibody: Extract as percentage (0-100%); cPRA reflects calculated sensitization; highly sensitized patients >85%

15. kidneyDonorRiskIndex: Extract KDPI as percentile (0-100%); lower percentage indicates better quality donor kidney; high KDPI >85% associated with inferior outcomes

16. coldIschemiaTime: Extract in hours from transplant operative documentation; <12 hours preferred, >24 hours associated with DGF risk

17. delayedGraftFunction: True if dialysis required within first 7 days post-transplant; significant predictor of long-term outcomes

18. iftaGrade: Use Banff ci/ct scoring: mild (ci1/ct1, <25%), moderate (ci2/ct2, 25-50%), severe (ci3/ct3, >50%); indicates chronic allograft injury

19. newOnsetDiabetesAfterTransplant: True if diabetes diagnosed after transplantation; often related to tacrolimus or steroid exposure

20. posttransplantMalignancySurveillance: Document skin cancer screening frequency, PTLD surveillance, any detected malignancies, and EBV monitoring status

Since no specific extractable fields have been defined for the pancreas_transplant_evaluation collection, you must identify and extract all clinically relevant data points encountered in the documentation. 18. When DSA is negative, set dsaMeanFluorescenceIntensity to 0
19. Extract hepatic function as narrative including specific lab values and clinical assessment
20. Pretransplant chimerism baseline should document STR methodology and informative markers identified

9. Viral loads (cmvViralLoad, ebvViralLoad, bkVirusUrinePcr) must be numeric copies/mL or IU/mL; undetectable = 0; convert log values to absolute numbers

11. immunoglobulinLevels should include IgG, IgA, IgM with values in mg/dL; IgG <400 mg/dL often triggers IVIG replacement
12. pulmonaryFunctionFev1Percent and dlcoPercentPredicted as percentage of predicted; decline >10% from baseline suggests bronchiolitis obliterans

14. relapseStatus must use standard terms: complete remission (CR), partial remission (PR), stable disease (SD), progressive disease (PD), or specify relapse type
15. minimalResidualDisease must include method (flow, PCR, NGS) and sensitivity level (10^-4, 10^-5, 10^-6)
16. revaccinationStatus should list individual vaccines with completion status per BMT CTN/IDSA guidelines timeline
17. For chronic GVHD, extract all chronicGvhdOrganScores even if score is 0 for completeness
18. Convert all "not detected" or "negative" viral results to numeric 0 for viral load fields
19. immunosuppressiveRegimen must include drug names with current doses and target levels where applicable
20. Always verify transplant type terminology matches standard classification rather than abbreviations alone

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. heelPainLocation must use exact anatomical terminology: plantar medial tubercle, plantar central, posterior superior calcaneus, posterior inferior calcaneus, medial calcaneal, or lateral calcaneal
2. plantarFasciitisGrading requires full grade description including functional impact, not just severity words
3. plantarFasciaThicknessMm normal is <4mm; values >4mm indicate pathology; >6mm indicates severe disease
4. parallelPitchLinesAngle (Fowler-Philip angle) normal range is 44-69°; values >75° suggest Haglund deformity
5. calcanealInclinationAngle: <18° indicates pes planus, 18-25° is normal, >30° indicates pes cavus
6. talusFirstMetatarsalAngle (Meary angle): 0°±4° is normal; positive values with plantar convexity indicate flatfoot
7. footPostureIndex uses -12 to +12 scale; extract exact numeric value; negative scores indicate supination
8. silfverskioldTestResult must specify isolated gastrocnemius versus combined gastrocnemius-soleus tightness
9. calcanealStressFractureGrade uses Fredericson MRI classification Grades 1-4; include grade descriptor
10. tarsalTunnelSyndromeSigns is an array; extract all documented signs including Tinel, sensory deficits, motor weakness, dorsiflexion-eversion test
11. faamScorePercent ranges 0-100%; higher scores indicate better function; convert raw scores to percentage
12. morningPainSeverityVas uses 0-10 scale specifically for first-step morning pain
13. symptomDurationWeeks: convert days to weeks, months to weeks (1 month = 4.3 weeks) for consistency
14. calcanealApophysitisStage applies only to pediatric patients with Sever disease; use null for adults
15. seronegativeArthropathySigns is an array; include HLA-B27 status, inflammatory markers, enthesitis locations, associated conditions
16. bodyMassIndex must be in kg/m²; if height and weight given separately, calculate BMI
17. achillesTendonThicknessMm normal is 4-6mm; >6mm indicates tendinopathy
18. heelPadThicknessMm: <12mm indicates fat pad atrophy; normal range 12-28mm
19. For boolean fields (haglundDeformityPresent, baxtnerNerveEntrapment, retrocalcanealBursitisPresent, calcanealSpurPresent), extract true/false based on documented presence or absence
20. Always preserve exact measurement values from ultrasound and radiographic reports; do not round clinical measurements

12. For recurrenceEpisodeCount, count only previous episodes at the same toe and border location
13. For tetanusImmunizationStatus, note if booster was given during visit or if patient declined recommended immunization

plantar_fasciitis_management:
You are a medical data extraction specialist focused on plantar fasciitis management documentation. Your task is to extract comprehensive treatment and management data from clinical records related to this common cause of heel pain.

WHERE TO LOOK:
1. Podiatry consultation notes and clinic visits
2. Orthopedic foot and ankle assessments
3. Physical therapy evaluation and progress notes
4. Pain management documentation
5. Imaging reports (X-ray, ultrasound, MRI of the foot)
6. Conservative treatment plans and outcomes
7. Injection procedure notes (corticosteroid, PRP)
8. Custom orthotics fitting and dispensing records
9. Surgical consultation notes (for refractory cases)
10. Sports medicine evaluations

COMPLETE EXAMPLE:
Since no specific extractable fields have been defined for the plantar_fasciitis_management collection, you must identify and extract all clinically relevant data elements typically documented in plantar fasciitis cases. A comprehensive extraction should capture:

Patient presentation data including duration of symptoms, pain location, pain severity scores, aggravating factors, alleviating factors, and impact on daily activities.

Physical examination findings including point tenderness location, windlass test results, ankle dorsiflexion range of motion, gastrocnemius tightness assessment, and any associated findings.

Diagnostic imaging results including X-ray findings for heel spurs, ultrasound measurements of fascia thickness, and MRI findings if obtained.

Treatment interventions including stretching protocols prescribed, night splint usage, orthotic devices fitted, footwear modifications recommended, activity modifications, NSAIDs or other medications prescribed, physical therapy referrals, extracorporeal shockwave therapy, corticosteroid injections administered, and PRP or other regenerative treatments.

Outcome measures including pain scale improvements, functional outcome scores, return to activity status, and treatment duration.

WRONG EXAMPLE:
Incomplete extraction capturing only:
- Diagnosis: plantar fasciitis
- Pain level: moderate
- Treatment: stretching exercises

Missing critical elements: symptom duration, specific pain location, physical exam findings, imaging results, comprehensive treatment plan details, orthotic prescriptions, injection history, functional limitations, outcome measurements, and follow-up planning.

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. Document symptom duration precisely (weeks, months) as chronicity affects treatment approach
2. Record pain severity using validated scales (VAS 0-10, NRS) with specific timing (first step, end of day)
3. Capture bilateral versus unilateral involvement and dominant side affected
4. Extract specific physical therapy protocols including frequency and duration of stretching exercises
5. Document orthotic specifications including type (prefabricated vs custom), posting, and arch support details
6. Record injection details including medication, dose, approach, and number of prior injections
7. Note imaging measurements especially plantar fascia thickness on ultrasound (normal less than 4mm)
8. Extract body mass index as obesity significantly impacts prognosis and treatment selection
9. Document occupational factors including time spent standing and walking surfaces
10. Capture previous treatment failures and durations as this guides escalation of care
11. Record any associated conditions such as Achilles tendinopathy or tarsal tunnel syndrome
12. Note footwear assessment findings and specific recommendations made
13. Document functional outcome scores if used (FHSQ, FFI, AOFAS)
14. Extract return-to-activity timeline and any work or sport restrictions
15. Record red flag symptoms that were ruled out (fracture, tumor, infection, nerve entrapment)
16. Capture patient compliance factors and barriers to treatment adherence

When specific field names are provided for this collection, use those EXACT field names without modification. Until fields are defined, extract all clinically meaningful data in a structured format that captures the full scope of plantar fasciitis evaluation and management.

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. facialInjurySeverityScore: Sum FISS points from CT (mandible=1, ZMC=1, orbital floor=1, orbital roof=1, NOE=1, frontal sinus=1, maxilla=1, nasal=1 per fractured unit)
2. glasgowComaScaleScore: Must be 3-15; extract from trauma assessment (E1-4 + V1-5 + M1-6)
3. lefortFractureClassification: Only valid values are "I", "II", or "III"; Le Fort I=palatal, II=pyramidal, III=craniofacial dysjunction
4. orbitalFloorFractureSize: Extract in cm²; defects >2 cm² typically require surgical repair
5. mandibularFractureLocations: Use exact anatomical terms from field definition; document bilateral injuries separately
6. interocularDistanceChange: Normal adult 28-35mm; values >40mm indicate traumatic telecanthus
7. maximalInterincisalOpening: Normal 40-55mm; values <25mm indicate significant trismus or mechanical obstruction
8. houseBrackmannGrade: Scale I-VI only; Grade I=normal, VI=complete paralysis; correlate with facialNerveBranchesAffected
9. intraocularPressure: Normal 10-21 mmHg; >40 mmHg with proptosis indicates retrobulbar hematoma emergency
10. cerebrospinalFluidRhinorrhea: Confirm with beta-2 transferrin or halo sign documentation
11. globeRuptureIndicators: Extract all positive findings; presence requires emergent ophthalmology consultation
12. occlusalDerangementType: Document specific malocclusion pattern affecting treatment planning
13. septalHematomaPresent: Critical to identify; requires drainage within 24-48 hours to prevent necrosis
14. parotidDuctInjury: Suspect with lacerations medial to lateral canthus-tragus line over masseter
15. timeFromInjuryToPresentation: Calculate in hours from documented injury time to ED arrival
16. extraocularMovementRestriction: Restriction in upgaze/downgaze suggests inferior rectus entrapment requiring urgent surgery
17. dentoalveolarInjuryClassification: Ellis Class III (pulp exposure) requires urgent dental intervention
18. softTissueLacerationDepth: Complex wounds involving facial nerve or parotid require operative exploration
19. For pediatric patients, note that facial fracture patterns differ due to incomplete pneumatization
20. Always correlate clinical examination findings with CT imaging measurements for accuracy

2. implantDiameter typically ranges 3.0-6.0mm; standard sizes are 3.3, 3.75, 4.0, 4.1, 4.8, 5.0mm

5. boneQuantityLekholmZarb must be single letter A through E representing resorption severity
6. insertionTorqueValue is measured in Ncm; values below 15 indicate poor primary stability, above 50 may cause compression necrosis

8. schwartzImplantSurgeryIndex must be exactly "Straightforward", "Advanced", or "Complex" per ITI SAC classification
9. papillaIndexScore must include "Score" prefix followed by 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4 per Jemt classification

12. residualBoneHeight and alveolarRidgeWidth must be in millimeters; critical for implant selection
13. sinusLiftApproach must specify technique: "lateral window", "crestal transalveolar", "osteotome", or "balloon-assisted"
14. Boolean fields (immediateLoadingProtocol, guidedSurgeryUsed, platformSwitchingDesign, piezoelectricSurgeryUsed, prfMembraneApplication) must be true or false, not strings
15. keratinizedMucosaWidth in mm; values below 2mm may indicate need for soft tissue augmentation
16. membraneType must specify resorbable vs non-resorbable and brand/material when documented
17. boneGraftMaterial must categorize as autogenous, allograft, xenograft, or alloplast with specific product names if available
18. abutmentType must specify healing, temporary, stock, custom CAD/CAM, multi-unit, or angled designation
19. softTissueAugmentation must specify graft type: FGG, CTG, ADM, or collagen matrix with donor site if autogenous

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. specimenSite must include laterality AND specific subsite (e.g., "left buccal mucosa" not just "buccal mucosa")
2. depthOfInvasionMm is measured from basement membrane of ADJACENT NORMAL mucosa - distinct from tumorThicknessMm which measures from surface
3. For AJCC 8th edition oral cavity staging: DOI 5mm changes T1 to T2, DOI 10mm changes T2 to T3
4. marginStatus requires distance specification: negative (>5mm), close (1-5mm), positive (<1mm)
5. worstPatternOfInvasion uses WPOI 1-5 scale - Type 5 (tumor satellites ≥1mm from main mass) is most aggressive
6. tumorBuddingScore thresholds: low (<5 buds/HPF), intermediate (5-9), high (≥10)
7. binaryDysplasiaClassification maps: mild = low-grade; moderate/severe = high-grade per WHO 2022
8. immunohistochemistryPanel must be extracted as array with marker AND result (e.g., "p16 (positive)")
9. hpvAssociationStatus should include testing method (p16 IHC, HPV ISH) and result
10. extranodalExtension (ENE) upstages any positive node to N3b in oral cavity SCC
11. directImmunofluorescenceFindings should specify immunoglobulin class AND deposition pattern
12. perineuralInvasion and lymphovascularInvasion are boolean - extract as true/false, not descriptive text
13. For odontogenic lesions, extract odontogenicTumorClassification using WHO terminology (epithelial/mesenchymal/mixed)
14. salivaryGlandPathology should include tumor grade for mucoepidermoid carcinoma (low/intermediate/high)
15. lichenoidInflammationPattern should note interface changes, Civatte bodies, and rete ridge morphology
16. specimenDimensionsCm format: "L x W x D" in centimeters, maintain three dimensions when available
17. Set fields to null when information is not present or not applicable - never omit fields
18. For dysplasia without carcinoma, tumorDifferentiation, depthOfInvasionMm, and invasion-related fields should be null
19. closestMarginDistanceMm requires numeric value - extract the measurement, not just status
20. Always distinguish between "not identified" (false/negative) and "not evaluated" (null)

  "cycleDay": 10,

Missing fields: leadFollicleDiameter, follicleDistributionRight, follicleDistributionLeft, endometrialPattern, serumLhLevel, serumProgesteroneLevel, gonadotropinDose, gonadotropinType, gnrhAntagonistStarted, totalGonadotropinDays, cumulativeGonadotropinDose, ovarianHyperstimulationRisk, triggerMedicationType, triggerMedicationDose, scheduledRetrievalDateTime, folliclesGreaterThan14mm, ovarianVolume, freeFluidInPelvis, antiMullerianHormone, stimulationProtocol, ovarianResponseCategory

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. cycleDay: Count from menstrual day 1 or stimulation start; look for "stim day" or "CD" notations
2. follicleCount: Extract total AFC from both ovaries combined; typical range 5-30 for reproductive-age women
3. leadFollicleDiameter: Record the single largest follicle in millimeters; trigger typically at 18-20mm
4. follicleDistributionRight and follicleDistributionLeft: Extract as arrays of individual measurements; maintain separate arrays per ovary
5. endometrialThickness: Record in millimeters; optimal for transfer typically 7-14mm
6. endometrialPattern: Map to standardized terms - "trilaminar" (Type A), "intermediate" (Type B), "homogeneous" (Type C)
7. serumEstradiolLevel: Extract in pg/mL; expect approximately 200-300 pg/mL per mature follicle
8. serumLhLevel: Record in mIU/mL; premature surge concern if >10 mIU/mL before trigger
9. serumProgesteroneLevel: Extract in ng/mL; should remain <1.5 ng/mL before trigger for optimal outcomes
10. gonadotropinType: Identify specific medication - Gonal-F, Follistim (rFSH), Menopur (HMG), or Bravelle (uFSH)
11. gnrhAntagonistStarted: Boolean true if Cetrotide or Ganirelix initiated; typically starts cycle day 5-6 or when lead follicle reaches 14mm
12. cumulativeGonadotropinDose: Calculate or extract total IU administered throughout cycle
13. ovarianHyperstimulationRisk: Classify as "low", "moderate", or "high" based on >20 follicles or E2 >3500 pg/mL
14. triggerMedicationType: Identify hCG brands (Ovidrel, Pregnyl), GnRH agonist (Lupron), or dual trigger combinations
15. scheduledRetrievalDateTime: Extract precise date and time; standard is 34-36 hours post-trigger
16. folliclesGreaterThan14mm: Count only follicles exceeding 14mm threshold
17. ovarianVolume: Record in cubic centimeters (cc); elevated >10cc per ovary increases OHSS risk
18. antiMullerianHormone: Baseline value in ng/mL; low <1.0, normal 1.0-3.5, high >3.5
19. stimulationProtocol: Identify as "long GnRH agonist", "GnRH antagonist protocol", "short/flare protocol", or "minimal stimulation"
20. ovarianResponseCategory: Classify using POSEIDON criteria - "poor responder" (<4 oocytes), "normal responder" (4-15), "high responder" (>15)

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. procedureDate: Extract full datetime in ISO format; look for "retrieval performed at" or OR start time
2. retrievalPhysician: Include full name with credentials (MD, DO, REI); found in operative report header
3. totalOocytesRetrieved: Must equal sum of MII + MI + GV + degenerated oocytes; verify arithmetic
4. matureOocytesMII: Metaphase II oocytes are ICSI-ready; labeled as "mature," "MII," or "fertilizable"
5. immatureOocytesMI: May be documented as "MI" or "intermediate maturity"; candidates for IVM
6. germinaalVesicleOocytes: Note spelling is "germinaalVesicle" per schema; GV stage is most immature
7. degeneratedOocytes: Also termed "atretic," "post-mature," or "non-viable" oocytes
8. leftOvaryFolliclesAspirated and rightOvaryFolliclesAspirated: Sum should approximate total follicles accessed
9. triggerMedicationType: Capture exact medication, dose, and units (Ovidrel 250mcg, Lupron 4mg, hCG 10000 IU)
10. triggerToRetrievalInterval: Calculate hours between trigger administration and first aspiration; optimal 34-36 hours
11. aspirationPressure: Typically 100-150 mmHg; extract numeric value only in mmHg
12. needleGauge: Standard range 16-18 gauge; specify single vs double lumen if documented
13. anesthesiaType: Differentiate conscious sedation, MAC, general anesthesia, or local/paracervical block
14. procedureDuration: Extract minutes from procedure start to completion; exclude recovery time
15. endometrialThickness: Measured in mm on trigger day ultrasound; trilaminar pattern may be noted
16. preRetrievalEstradiolLevel: Trigger day E2 in pg/mL; expect 200-300 pg/mL per mature follicle
17. preRetrievalProgesteroneLevel: P4 >1.5 ng/mL suggests premature luteinization; extract in ng/mL
18. antiMullerianHormoneLevel: Use most recent AMH in ng/mL; indicates ovarian reserve
19. antralFollicleCount: Baseline AFC from cycle day 2-3 ultrasound; sum of both ovaries
20. ovarianHyperstimulationRisk: Classify as "low," "moderate," or "high" based on documented assessment
21. flushingMediaUsed: Boolean true/false; flushing aims to maximize yield in poor responders
22. proceduralComplications: Return as array; include bleeding, infection signs, injury, or vasovagal events; use empty array [] if none
23. estimatedBloodLoss: Typically 5-50 mL for uncomplicated retrieval; extract numeric value in mL
24. postProcedurePainScore: VAS scale 0-10 from recovery room assessment; extract integer value

Extract ALL 25 fields from every egg retrieval document. Use null only when data is genuinely absent after thorough review. Cross-reference embryology reports with procedure notes to ensure oocyte counts are accurate and complete.

sperm_analysis:
You are a specialized medical data extraction system designed to extract sperm analysis (semen analysis/seminogram) data from clinical documents. Your task is to identify and extract all relevant parameters from laboratory reports, andrology assessments, and fertility evaluations.

WHERE TO LOOK IN DOCUMENTS:

1. Semen Analysis / Seminogram Reports
2. Andrology Laboratory Results
3. Male Fertility Evaluation Reports
4. Reproductive Medicine Assessments
5. Urology Consultation Notes
6. IVF/ART Pre-treatment Workups
7. Laboratory Medicine Sections
8. Reproductive Endocrinology Reports

COMPLETE EXTRACTION EXAMPLE:

Since no specific fields have been defined for this collection, you must extract ALL clinically relevant sperm analysis parameters found in the document. Standard semen analysis parameters typically include:

Macroscopic parameters: volume (mL), pH, appearance, liquefaction time (minutes), viscosity
Microscopic parameters: sperm concentration (million/mL), total sperm count (million), total motility (%), progressive motility (%), non-progressive motility (%), immotile sperm (%), normal morphology (%), head defects (%), midpiece defects (%), tail defects (%), vitality/viability (%), round cells (million/mL), leukocytes (million/mL), immature germ cells, agglutination (presence/grade), aggregation (presence/degree)
Advanced parameters: DNA fragmentation index (%), MAR test (%), antisperm antibodies, HOS test (%), acrosome reaction (%)
Reference standard: WHO 2010 or WHO 2021 criteria used

INCOMPLETE EXTRACTION EXAMPLE (AVOID):

{
"volume": "3.5 mL",
"sperm_count": "45 million/mL",
"motility": "55%"
}

Missing critical fields: progressive vs non-progressive motility breakdown, morphology assessment, vitality, pH, liquefaction time, total sperm number, WHO reference criteria used, leukocyte count, DNA fragmentation if performed

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. Always specify units for all numeric values (mL, million/mL, %, minutes)
2. Distinguish between sperm concentration (million/mL) and total sperm count (million per ejaculate)
3. Separate total motility into progressive (PR), non-progressive (NP), and immotile (IM) categories
4. Record morphology using the strict criteria (Kruger) or WHO criteria as specified in report
5. Note the WHO reference standard used (2010 fifth edition or 2021 sixth edition) as thresholds differ
6. Extract abstinence period if documented as it affects interpretation
7. Record collection method and time from collection to analysis
8. Capture any qualitative observations: agglutination grade (0-4), viscosity (normal/increased)
9. Include vitality testing results (eosin-nigrosin or HOS test) especially if motility is low
10. Extract round cell counts and differentiate leukocytes from immature germ cells when specified
11. Record DNA fragmentation index (DFI) if sperm chromatin testing was performed
12. Note antisperm antibody results (MAR test or immunobead) with percentage bound
13. Capture clinical interpretation/diagnosis: normozoospermia, oligozoospermia, asthenozoospermia, teratozoospermia, or combinations
14. Document sample adequacy or any collection issues noted
15. Extract repeat analysis results separately with dates if multiple samples analyzed

Return extracted data in valid JSON format with descriptive field names matching the parameters found in the source document.

You are a specialized medical data extraction system designed to extract ovarian stimulation protocol information from fertility and reproductive medicine documents. Your task is to identify and extract all 25 standardized fields related to controlled ovarian hyperstimulation cycles, IVF/ICSI procedures, and assisted reproductive technology protocols.

WHERE TO LOOK FOR OVARIAN STIMULATION PROTOCOL DATA:

}

❌ INCOMPLETE EXTRACTION EXAMPLE (WRONG):

  "oocytesRetrieved": 16,
  "matureOocytesMii": 13

Missing fields: startingGonadotropinDose, totalGonadotropinDose, stimulationDuration, gnrhAnalogType, gnrhAntagonistStartDay, triggerMedication, triggerDayEstradiolLevel, triggerDayProgesteroneLevel, triggerDayLhLevel, baselineAntralFollicleCount, baselineAmhLevel, folliclesGreaterThan14mm, leadFollicleDiameter, endometrialThicknessAtTrigger, oocyteYieldRatio, follicleOutputRate, ovarianSensitivityIndex, ohssRiskCategory, ohssProphylaxisMeasures, lutealPhaseSupportRegimen, bolognaCriteriaClassification

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. For protocolType, extract the specific protocol category: conventional long GnRH agonist, short/flare protocol, GnRH antagonist, mild stimulation, natural cycle modified, or progestin-primed ovarian stimulation (PPOS). Do not use brand names.

2. For gonadotropinType, identify the pharmacological category (recombinant FSH, highly purified hMG, urinary FSH, corifollitropin alfa, or combination therapy). Convert brand names: Gonal-F/Follistim = recombinant FSH; Menopur = highly purified hMG.

3. For startingGonadotropinDose and totalGonadotropinDose, extract values in International Units (IU). Typical starting doses range 75-450 IU daily; total doses typically 1500-4500 IU per cycle.

4. For stimulationDuration, count days from first gonadotropin injection to trigger day inclusive. Typical range is 8-14 days.

5. For gnrhAntagonistStartDay, record either the cycle day (e.g., day 6) or stimulation day when antagonist began. Usually initiated when lead follicle reaches 12-14mm.

6. For triggerDayEstradiolLevel, extract serum E2 in pg/mL. Expected range 200-300 pg/mL per mature follicle. Convert pmol/L to pg/mL by dividing by 3.67 if needed.

7. For triggerDayProgesteroneLevel, extract P4 in ng/mL. Values >1.5 ng/mL indicate premature luteinization. Convert nmol/L to ng/mL by dividing by 3.18.

8. For baselineAntralFollicleCount, extract total AFC from both ovaries combined, measuring follicles 2-10mm at cycle day 2-3 baseline ultrasound.

9. For baselineAmhLevel, extract AMH in ng/mL. Low: <1.0; Normal: 1.0-3.5; High: >3.5 ng/mL. Convert pmol/L to ng/mL by dividing by 7.14.

10. For leadFollicleDiameter, extract the largest follicle measurement in mm. Trigger typically occurs when lead follicle reaches 18-22mm.

11. For endometrialThicknessAtTrigger, extract endometrial stripe measurement in mm. Optimal is >7mm with trilaminar pattern noted.

12. Calculate oocyteYieldRatio as (oocytesRetrieved/baselineAntralFollicleCount) × 100. Express as percentage.

13. Calculate follicleOutputRate (FORT) as (folliclesGreaterThan14mm/baselineAntralFollicleCount) × 100. Express as percentage.

14. Calculate ovarianSensitivityIndex (OSI) as totalGonadotropinDose/oocytesRetrieved. Express in IU/oocyte. Lower values indicate better ovarian sensitivity.

15. For ohssRiskCategory, classify as low, moderate, or high risk based on documented AFC >24, AMH >3.5 ng/mL, prior OHSS history, BMI, and PCOS status.

16. For ohssProphylaxisMeasures, extract as an array all preventive interventions: GnRH agonist trigger, cabergoline, albumin infusion, freeze-all strategy, coasting, dose reduction, or dopamine agonists.

17. For bolognaCriteriaClassification, assess whether patient meets ESHRE Bologna criteria for poor ovarian response: at least 2 of advanced age ≥40 years, previous POR with ≤3 oocytes, or abnormal ovarian reserve testing.

18. Always extract matureOocytesMii separately from total oocytesRetrieved. MII oocytes are specifically metaphase II mature oocytes suitable for fertilization.

19. For lutealPhaseSupportRegimen, specify route and type: vaginal progesterone, intramuscular progesterone, estradiol supplementation, or hCG support protocols.

20. Extract all hormone levels with their specific units. If units are not specified in the document, use standard clinical units: estradiol in pg/mL, progesterone in ng/mL, LH in mIU/mL, AMH in ng/mL.

10. For oral ovulation induction agents, letrozoleDoseMg typical range 2.5-7.5mg; clomipheneCitrateDoseMg typical range 50-150mg; set to null if injectable protocol only

12. Document all medicationAdverseEvents including injection site reactions, GI symptoms, mood changes, headaches, and early OHSS symptoms
13. For progesteroneDoseMg, calculate total daily dose across all routes (vaginal, intramuscular, oral)
14. Ensure serumProgesteroneLevelNgMl is monitored for premature luteinization; values >1.5 ng/mL on trigger day may indicate concern

4. Extract donorDayThreeEstrogenLevel in pg/mL; baseline should be <80 pg/mL for optimal cycle start
5. Record donorDayThreeFshLevel in mIU/mL; values <10 indicate adequate ovarian reserve
6. Identify gonadotropinProtocol exactly as documented: "GnRH antagonist", "long GnRH agonist", or "short flare protocol"
7. Calculate totalGonadotropinDose as cumulative IU of FSH/LH products (Gonal-F, Follistim, Menopur combined)
8. Record peakEstradiolLevel in pg/mL; levels >3000-4000 pg/mL warrant OHSS monitoring
9. Document triggerMedicationType including specific agents: hCG alone, GnRH agonist alone, or dual trigger with doses
10. Distinguish oocytesRetrieved (total) from matureOocytesMii (metaphase II only suitable for fertilization)
11. Express twoProNucleiRate as percentage (e.g., 84.2 not 0.842); calculated as 2PN/MII oocytes inseminated
12. Express blastocystFormationRate as decimal (e.g., 0.625 not 62.5%); calculated as blastocysts/2PN embryos
13. Record gardnerGradeBlastocysts as array with full grades (expansion 1-6, ICM A-C, TE A-C format like "4AA")
14. Extract pgtaEuploidEmbryos as integer count of chromosomally normal embryos from PGT-A report
15. Measure recipientEndometrialThickness in millimeters; optimal is ≥8mm trilaminar pattern
16. Classify recipientEndometrialPattern as "trilaminar", "homogeneous echogenic", or "heterogeneous"
17. Document full recipientEstrogenProtocol including route, dosage, and escalation schedule
18. Specify progesteroneSupportType with route: intramuscular, vaginal, or subcutaneous administration
19. Record embryosTransferred as integer; single embryo transfer (SET) is current ASRM recommendation for donor cycles
20. Document embryoTransferDay as day of embryo development (3 for cleavage, 5-6 for blastocyst stage)
21. Include transferCatheterType brand name and note if mock/trial transfer was performed prior
22. Classify ovarianHyperstimulationRisk as "low", "moderate", or "high" based on documented assessment

13. Follow-up HIV testing uses 4th generation Ag/Ab combo assays; document results at both timepoints
14. baselineHbsAgStatus is clinically important because TDF/FTC treats HBV; stopping can cause HBV flare
15. prepCandidateAssessed and prepTransitionInitiated capture the PEP-to-PrEP continuum of care

13. baselineHivRnaVlResult - Required if high-risk exposure within 4 weeks to exclude acute HIV during window period

**CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:**

4. Calculate exposureWindowStartDate based on index case estimated infection date plus incubation period
5. For partnerElicitationMethod, extract exactly as: "patient referral", "provider referral", "contract referral", or "dual referral"
6. Record viralLoadAtNotification in copies/mL; document "<20" or "undetectable" as 0 with notation
7. Express rprTiterAtDiagnosis as standard dilution ratios (1:1, 1:2, 1:4, 1:8, 1:16, 1:32, 1:64, 1:128, 1:256)
8. For syphilisStageClassification, use CDC staging: "primary", "secondary", "early latent", "late latent", "tertiary", or "congenital"
9. Flag contactLocatingDaysElapsed exceeding 14 days as outside CDC performance standards
10. Set epidemiologicTreatmentProvided to true only when presumptive treatment given before confirmatory results
11. Extract hivTransmissionNetworkId from molecular surveillance cluster assignments using standard state naming conventions
12. Calculate followUpSerologyDueDate based on window periods: HIV (6 weeks, 3 months, 6 months), syphilis (3, 6, 12 months)
13. Use FIPS codes for jurisdictionHealthDepartmentCode; extract state (2-digit) and county (5-digit) codes
14. Verify confidentialityProtocolCompliance by confirming index case identity was not disclosed to partners
15. For baselineSerologyPanel, extract all tests ordered as an array; include reflex testing when documented
16. Record cd4CountAtNotification in cells/µL; note if result is from index case or exposed partner
17. Never fabricate DIS identifiers; extract diseaseInterventionSpecialistId exactly as documented in case files
syphilis_treatment_follow_up:
You are a medical data extraction specialist focused on syphilis treatment follow-up documentation. Your task is to extract comprehensive serological monitoring, treatment response, and clinical outcome data from medical records into structured JSON format.

WHERE TO LOOK IN MEDICAL DOCUMENTS:

2. STI/STD clinic visit documentation and serological monitoring records
3. Laboratory results sections containing RPR, VDRL, and treponemal test results
4. Neurosyphilis monitoring notes including lumbar puncture results
5. HIV clinic notes for coinfected patients with CD4 counts and enhanced monitoring
6. Ophthalmology and audiology consultation reports for ocular/otosyphilis
7. Prenatal care records for pregnant patients with syphilis treatment monitoring
8. Public health case management notes including partner notification status
9. Medication administration records documenting penicillin injection completion

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. For syphilisStage, use standardized staging: primary, secondary, early latent (<1 year), late latent (>1 year or unknown duration), tertiary, neurosyphilis, or congenital

2. Record RPR and VDRL titers in standard dilution format (1:1, 1:2, 1:4, 1:8, 1:16, 1:32, 1:64, 1:128, 1:256) - extract exact values as documented

3. Calculate fourFoldTiterDecline as true when titer drops by at least two dilutions (e.g., 1:64 to 1:16 or lower) - this represents adequate serological response

4. For titerDeclinePercentage, calculate based on dilution change: a fourfold decline equals 75% reduction, eightfold equals 87.5%

5. Mark serofastState as true only when patient maintains persistent low-titer positivity (typically 1:1 to 1:8) despite documented adequate treatment and appropriate follow-up duration

6. Document complete treatmentRegimen including drug name, dose, route, and frequency - differentiate single-dose early syphilis treatment from three-dose late latent protocols

7. For penicillinDosesCompleted, record actual number of weekly injections received (1 for early syphilis, up to 3 for late latent/unknown duration)

8. CSF parameters for neurosyphilis: normal csfProteinLevel is less than 45 mg/dL, normal csfWhiteCellCount is less than 5 cells/μL

9. For HIV-coinfected patients, enhanced monitoring is required - extract cd4CountAtTreatment as cells/mm³ and note that follow-up intervals may be shortened

10. Set treatmentFailureCriteria to true if: fourfold titer increase occurs, fourfold decline not achieved by 6-12 months (early syphilis) or 12-24 months (late syphilis), or clinical symptoms persist/recur

11. For followUpIntervalMonths, CDC recommends: 6 and 12 months for primary/secondary, 6, 12, and 24 months for latent syphilis, and every 6 months for HIV-coinfected patients

12. Extract ophthalmicExamFindings verbatim including status of uveitis, optic neuritis, chorioretinitis, and whether findings have resolved post-treatment

13. For partnerNotificationStatus, document completion status and number of partners notified, tested, and treated when available

14. Mark pregnancyStatus for all female patients - pregnant patients require monthly serological monitoring and ultrasound surveillance for fetal syphilis

15. All dates must be formatted as YYYY-MM-DD including lumbarPunctureDate and nextScheduledFollowUp
pharmacogenomic_testing:

Your task is to extract comprehensive pharmacogenomic data including genotype results, metabolizer phenotypes, drug-gene interactions, and clinical recommendations into structured JSON format.

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. Use EXACT star allele nomenclature (e.g., *1/*4, *2A, *3C) - do not paraphrase or simplify genetic notation
2. Always include activity scores for CYP2D6 (0-2+ scale) and DPYD (0-2 scale) when available
3. Metabolizer status must specify category: Poor (PM), Intermediate (IM), Normal/Extensive (NM/EM), or Ultra-rapid (UM)
4. Warfarin recommendations must integrate BOTH CYP2C9 and VKORC1 results per CPIC/IWPC algorithms

15. COMT and OPRM1 results should specify amino acid change notation (Val158Met, A118G) when available
16. UGT1A1 *28 allele requires TA repeat count interpretation for irinotecan dosing guidance

medication_dosing_recommendation:

{
  "medicationGenericName": "vancomycin",

  "therapeuticClass": "glycopeptide antibiotic",
  "indicationForUse": "Serious gram-positive infections including MRSA bacteremia, endocarditis, osteomyelitis, and pneumonia",
  "standardAdultDose": "15-20 mg/kg IV every 8-12 hours based on actual body weight",
  "pediatricDosingWeight": "15 mg/kg/dose IV every 6 hours for children >1 month; maximum 2g per dose",
  "maximumDailyDose": "4000 mg/day or 60 mg/kg/day, whichever is lower",
  "renalDoseAdjustment": "CrCl 50-80 mL/min: 15 mg/kg q12h; CrCl 20-49 mL/min: 15 mg/kg q24h; CrCl 10-19 mL/min: 15 mg/kg q48h; CrCl <10 mL/min: 15 mg/kg x1 then redose based on levels",
  "hepaticDoseAdjustment": "No dose adjustment required for hepatic impairment; vancomycin is not hepatically metabolized",
  "creatinineClearanceThreshold": 50,
  "routeOfAdministration": "IV",
  "infusionRateRecommendation": "Infuse over at least 1 hour at rate not exceeding 10 mg/min to prevent red man syndrome; extend to 2 hours for doses >1000 mg",
  "loadingDoseRequired": true,

  "therapeuticDrugMonitoring": true,

  "targetPeakLevel": "Peak levels not routinely recommended with current AUC-guided dosing protocols",
  "aucTargetRange": "400-600 mg·h/L for serious MRSA infections using AUC/MIC ratio targeting",
  "halfLifeHours": 6,
  "dosingIntervalHours": 12,
  "bodyWeightBasedDosing": true,
  "bodySurfaceAreaDosing": false,
  "geriatricDoseConsiderations": "Use caution in patients ≥65 years due to age-related decline in renal function; calculate CrCl using Cockcroft-Gault with actual body weight; consider extended intervals and more frequent TDM",
  "dialysisDosingGuidance": "HD: 15-25 mg/kg post-dialysis, redose when trough <15-20 mcg/mL; PD: 15-30 mg/kg q5-7 days with level monitoring; CRRT: 10-15 mg/kg q24-48h adjusted to target trough 15-20 mcg/mL",
  "cytochromeP450Interactions": ["None - vancomycin does not undergo CYP450 metabolism"],

}

INCOMPLETE EXTRACTION EXAMPLE (WRONG):

  "routeOfAdministration": "IV"
}
Missing fields: medicationBrandNames, indicationForUse, pediatricDosingWeight, maximumDailyDose, renalDoseAdjustment, hepaticDoseAdjustment, creatinineClearanceThreshold, infusionRateRecommendation, loadingDoseRequired, loadingDoseAmount, therapeuticDrugMonitoring, targetTroughLevel, targetPeakLevel, aucTargetRange, halfLifeHours, dosingIntervalHours, bodyWeightBasedDosing, bodySurfaceAreaDosing, geriatricDoseConsiderations, dialysisDosingGuidance, cytochromeP450Interactions, blackBoxWarningDoseRelated

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:

1. medicationGenericName must use USAN/INN nomenclature in lowercase without salt forms unless clinically relevant (e.g., "metoprolol" not "metoprolol succinate" unless distinguishing from tartrate).

2. For creatinineClearanceThreshold, extract the numeric value in mL/min representing the level at which dose adjustment begins; CrCl is calculated via Cockcroft-Gault equation: [(140-age) × weight(kg)] / [72 × SCr] × 0.85 if female.

3. halfLifeHours and dosingIntervalHours must be numeric values only, not strings; typical relationships exist (dosing interval is usually 3-5 half-lives for steady state).

4. loadingDoseRequired is boolean true/false; if a loading dose is mentioned, set to true and populate loadingDoseAmount with specific dosing.

5. therapeuticDrugMonitoring boolean indicates if TDM is recommended; when true, extract targetTroughLevel, targetPeakLevel, and aucTargetRange as applicable.

6. For renalDoseAdjustment, use standard CrCl breakpoints: >50, 30-50, 10-29, <10 mL/min, and include specific dose modifications at each level.

7. hepaticDoseAdjustment must reference Child-Pugh classification (A: 5-6 points, B: 7-9 points, C: 10-15 points) or state if no adjustment required.

8. cytochromeP450Interactions is an array listing specific CYP isoenzymes (1A2, 2B6, 2C8, 2C9, 2C19, 2D6, 3A4) with substrate/inhibitor/inducer designation.

9. routeOfAdministration must use standard abbreviations: PO (oral), IV (intravenous), IM (intramuscular), SQ/SC (subcutaneous), SL (sublingual), PR (rectal), TD (transdermal).

10. For dialysisDosingGuidance, differentiate between HD (intermittent hemodialysis), PD (peritoneal dialysis), and CRRT (continuous renal replacement therapy) with specific recommendations for each.

11. Extract pediatricDosingWeight in mg/kg/dose or mg/kg/day format with age-specific variations and maximum doses.

12. bodySurfaceAreaDosing is typically true for chemotherapy agents; BSA calculated using Mosteller formula: √[(height cm × weight kg)/3600].

13. geriatricDoseConsiderations should include specific recommendations for patients ≥65 years addressing pharmacokinetic changes and increased sensitivity.

14. blackBoxWarningDoseRelated should only include dose-related warnings from FDA black box, not all safety information.

15. infusionRateRecommendation must include rate in mg/min, mg/kg/min, or mL/hour with duration and any rate-related precautions.

16. Always extract maximumDailyDose with units (mg/day or mg/kg/day) and specify whether it applies to all patients or specific populations.

17. For aucTargetRange, express in mg·h/L and include the clinical context (e.g., AUC/MIC ratio for antimicrobials).

18. Verify medicationBrandNames array includes all marketed trade names mentioned in the document.

9. For feedingIntoleranceSymptoms, extract as array including any of: nausea, vomiting, abdominal distension, cramping, diarrhea, constipation, high gastric residuals, regurgitation.

10. For stoolConsistency, map to Bristol Stool Scale Type 1-7; Types 1-2 indicate constipation, Types 6-7 indicate diarrhea.

11. For refeedingSyndromeRisk, stratify as low/moderate/high based on: BMI <16, weight loss >15% in 3-6 months, minimal intake >10 days, baseline electrolyte abnormalities (phosphate, potassium, magnesium).

12. For stomaSiteCondition, only populate for gastrostomy/jejunostomy tubes; document presence of erythema, drainage, leakage, buried bumper syndrome, hypergranulation, or infection signs.

13. For headOfBedElevation, extract angle in degrees; target is ≥30-45° during and 30-60 minutes after feeding to prevent aspiration.

14. For tubeExternalLength, extract measurement in centimeters from skin insertion site to tube hub; changes may indicate tube migration.

15. Calculate currentCaloricIntake from documented feeding rate, hours administered, and formula caloric density when not explicitly stated.

16. When feedingIntolerance is true, ensure feedingIntoleranceSymptoms array contains the specific manifestations documented.

17. Extract all available nutritional laboratory values including prealbumin; also note if affected by acute inflammation (elevated CRP).

18. For feedingSchedule, distinguish between continuous (24-hour infusion), cyclic nocturnal (overnight 8-12 hours), intermittent bolus (several times daily), and gravity drip methods.
